items.title: First fixture thread for tone + lane coverage
items.body: Suggestion body for the first thread. Length is chosen to wrap roughly two lines under the 1280px capture viewport so .feedback-thread-body line-height + max-width rules are exercised.
items.author: Owner-Fixture

replies.body: First reply on the right lane. Author Alpha receives tone 1 per the toneForAuthor round-robin assignment.
replies.author: Alpha

replies.body: Second reply on the left lane. Carries a replyTo so .feedback-reply.is-left .feedback-reply-context chrome renders.
replies.author: Bravo
replies.replyToBody: First reply on the right lane. Author Alpha receives tone 1 per the toneForAuthor round-robin assignment.

replies.body: Third reply on the right lane. Carries a replyTo so .feedback-reply.is-right .feedback-reply-context chrome renders as well.
replies.author: Charlie
replies.replyToBody: First reply on the right lane. Author Alpha receives tone 1 per the toneForAuthor round-robin assignment.

replies.body: Fourth reply on the left lane. Author Delta receives tone 4 per the round-robin assignment.
replies.author: Delta

replies.body: Fifth reply on the right lane. Author Echo receives tone 5 — the last available tone in the .tone-N cycle.
replies.author: Echo

items.title: Second fixture thread for inter-thread spacing coverage
items.body: Second thread body. Two threads on the page exercise the .feedback-list inter-thread gap + .feedback-thread::after rail when repeated across siblings.
items.author: Second-Owner

replies.body: Single reply on the second thread. Keeps the second card from collapsing to its empty-replies layout so .feedback-replies gap still applies.
replies.author: Foxtrot
